steps.subtitle: A whole pizza is 360°. A quarter of it is a square corner at the centre. Drag the slider until the [central angle](teal) looks like a quarter slice, then check.

feedback.incorrect: A quarter of the circle is a square corner — 90°. Open the slice until the two sides make an L.

steps.revealText: A quarter slice is 90° — because the whole circle is 360°, and 360 ÷ 4 = 90.

steps.prompt: A sector is a slice of pizza

graphic.name: pizza
graphic.caption: A whole pizza is 360°. Cut from the centre and you get a slice — a sector. The wider the angle at the centre, the bigger the slice.

steps.body:
A [sector](teal) is a pie-slice of a circle, set by the [central angle](teal) at the centre.
The [arc](teal) is the curved crust along the edge of the slice, and the [radius](blue) is the straight side from the centre to the rim.
The whole lesson is one idea: a slice is just a fraction of the whole circle.

steps.subtitle: Drag the handle to change the [central angle](teal), and watch what fraction of the whole circle the [sector](teal) covers.
Each slice has the same [radius](blue); only the [central angle](teal) differs. A bigger angle is a bigger slice.
steps.instruction: Tap the slices from smallest to largest.
steps.doneNote: Right — the slice grows with the central angle: 60° < 90° < 150° < 210° < 300°.

steps.body:
A slice is the fraction [θ / 360](teal) of the whole circle, so it takes that same fraction of the circle's edge and of its area:
[arc length](teal) = (θ / 360) × 2π[r](blue),  and  [sector area](teal) = (θ / 360) × π[r](blue)².
A 90° slice of a [radius](blue)-6 circle has arc length 3π and area 9π.

steps.subtitle: A [sector](teal) has [central angle](teal) 90° in a circle of [radius](blue) 6. Select every statement that is true.

options.label: It is a quarter of the whole circle
It is half of the whole circle

feedback.correct: All three! 90° is 90/360 = ¼ of the circle. Arc = ¼ × 2π×6 = 3π, and area = ¼ × π×6² = 9π.
feedback.incorrect: 90° is a quarter, not a half. And the area keeps its π: ¼ × π × 6² = 9π, not 9.

problems.prompt: A sector has radius 6 and central angle 90°. Find its area (in terms of π).

feedback.incorrect: Sector area is the fraction θ/360 of the whole area, πr².

problems.prompt: A sector has radius 6 and central angle 120°. Find its arc length (in terms of π).

feedback.incorrect: Arc length is the fraction θ/360 of the whole circumference, 2πr.

problems.prompt: A sector has radius 10 and central angle 180°. Find its area (in terms of π).

feedback.incorrect: A 180° sector is half the circle, so take half of πr².

steps.intro: A [sector](teal) has [central angle](teal) 150° in a circle of [radius](blue) 6. Find its area in terms of π — one move at a time.

parts.prompt: First, what fraction of the whole circle is a 150° slice?

options.feedback: Yes — divide top and bottom by 30.

parts.note: The slice is 5/12 of the whole circle.

parts.prompt: The whole circle's area is πr² = π × 6². What is 6²?
Now take 5/12 of that whole area: (5/12) × 36 = ?
parts.note: The sector area is 15π — the fraction θ/360 of πr².

steps.doneNote: That's the method: find the fraction θ/360, then take that fraction of the whole circle. (150÷360) × π(6)² = 15π.
